what_it_does: Implements the Composition workspace: a master-detail browser for the project's catalog of producible kinds (MS_KINDS) that resolves each kind's variables against the current project context (MS_RESOLVE), shows a rendered specimen, exposes the full precedence trace for every variable, lists generated artifacts (MS_ARTIFACTS), surfaces relevant memory (MS_MEMORY), and lets the user pick or teach variant preferences before generating new artifacts.
observations: All domain logic is accessed through globals on window (MS_KINDS, MS_RESOLVE, MS_MEMORY, MS_ARTIFACTS, MS_VI_BRAIN, MS_PRODUCE, MS_TOAST, etc.) rather than imported modules.
Pure helpers are grouped under window.MS_COMPOSE so they can be unit-tested without a DOM.
The UI reuses a generic .dsys__ master-detail shell and only adds .comp-* classes for composition-specific layout.
Each resolved variable is rendered with a native <details> element that shows the resolver's precedence trace tier-by-tier.
Variant selection includes approve/avoid buttons that call MS_RESOLVE.noteVariant to shift future leaned defaults.
The workspace calls window.useArtifacts and window.useFeedbackStore to stay live as artifacts or feedback change.
A StreamHost and CandidateCard are conditionally rendered if their global components exist.
standouts: Live, per-variable resolver precedence trace UI.
Variant teaching that updates the leaned default and feeds back into future generations.
Shared context mapping via MS_VI_BRAIN.genContext to keep resolution and generation aligned.
Testable pure helpers separated from React components.
conventions: Functional React components with props destructured in the signature.
Global API modules hung off window.
CSS class naming mixes generic .dsys__* with local .comp-* prefixes.
Hooks aliased (useStateCO) to avoid shadowing or for clarity.
Conditional rendering based on the presence of global components and APIs.
concerns: Heavy dependency on globals means contracts are implicit and not type-checked.
noteVariant is defined inside CODetail and relies on closure over several window globals.
The useEffect that updates variant only depends on kind.id, not ctx, so context changes may not reset the variant.
In the toast message, noted may be undefined when approved is false, producing an awkward string.
No visible error handling if MS_RESOLVE.instance or MS_RESOLVE.explain fail.
notes: A user-facing composition studio that makes the generative type-to-instance pipeline inspectable and steerable.
questions: Where are MS_KINDS, MS_RESOLVE, MS_MEMORY, MS_ARTIFACTS, and MS_VI_BRAIN defined and what are their exact contracts?
What does KindSpecimen render and how does it consume kind/values/variant/seed?
What is the shape of the tweaks and project props passed into this workspace?
How does MS_RESOLVE.variantBias compute the leaned default and what does noteVariant persist?
What scoring does MS_MEMORY.recall use to rank memory hits?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it browses the catalog, resolves variables, shows traces and memory, and supports generation.
apparent_themes: Single source of truth between resolution and generation
Testable pure logic separated from UI
intention_signals: The file is trying to turn the opaque generative pipeline into an inspectable, interactive studio where a user can see why a part resolved the way it did, nudge future variants, and generate artifacts from the same context.
novelty: The combination of a live resolver precedence trace per variable with direct variant teaching that immediately updates the leaned default, all tied to a shared generative context.
contribution: Provides the interactive composition surface that exposes the catalog, renders resolved specimens, explains variable resolution, supports variant feedback, and connects artifacts, memory, and generation into one inspectable workspace.
summary_for_embedding: A React workspace that browses a catalog of producible design-system kinds, resolves their variables against project context, visualizes precedence traces, and supports variant teaching and artifact generation.
intention_for_embedding: To make the generative design system's type-to-instance pipeline visible, explainable, and steerable from a single composition UI.

symbols.does: Maps project tweak values into the resolver preference tier, delegating to MS_VI_BRAIN.genContext when available.
symbols.description: Builds the context object used by MS_RESOLVE so Composition resolves variables against the same preferences that the Vi console and streams use; falls back to a manual mapping when the brain module is absent.

symbols.does: Counts total bindings, sources, and unresolved variables for an instance.
symbols.description: Aggregates an instance's bindings into a small summary object displayed in the Variables section header.

symbols.does: Formats a value for display, truncating long strings and representing null or undefined.
symbols.description: Small formatter used throughout the workspace to render resolved values and trace entries consistently.

symbols.does: Renders the left-hand category list of producible kinds.
symbols.description: Navigable master pane that groups kinds by category, shows each kind's variable count and made count, and calls onPick when a kind is selected.

symbols.does: Renders one resolved variable with an expandable precedence trace.
symbols.description: Displays the variable label, type, resolved value, source, and a native details element containing the full resolver explanation from MS_RESOLVE.explain.

symbols.does: Renders the detail view for a selected kind, including specimen, variants, variables, instances, and memory.
symbols.description: Main detail component that resolves the kind against the project context, shows a rendered specimen, lets the user pick or teach variant preferences, lists resolved variables, generated artifacts, and relevant memory hits.

symbols.does: Records a prefer or avoid signal for a variant and updates the leaned default.
symbols.description: Closure inside CODetail that calls MS_RESOLVE.noteVariant and, on approval, moves the selected variant to the leaned default while showing a toast confirmation.

symbols.does: Selects a kind and reveals the detail pane on mobile.
symbols.description: Small handler in CompositionWorkspace that updates the selected kind id and toggles the mobile detail view.
